entries.title: Test Flakiness Tracker
entries.notes: Parses CI test output history from common CI providers to flag tests with rising failure variance. Buildable in 2-3 weeks by hooking into CI APIs and localizing pattern detection. The niche is narrow but nobody does flaky test prediction specifically, most tools only report past failures.
entries.pain: Developers waste hours debugging tests that fail randomly without understanding flakiness patterns or root causes.

entries.title: GraphQL Field Usage Analyzer
entries.notes: Injects into GraphQL playground or intercepts network to build a field usage heatmap over time. Ship as a devtools panel that shows dead-field candidates. GraphQL ecosystem tooling is still fragmented compared to REST ecosystem.
entries.pain: GraphQL developers maintain resolver code for fields nobody queries, accumulating schema bloat and wasted backend work.

entries.title: LinkedIn Employee Export
entries.notes: Extracts visible employee names, titles, and headcount from any LinkedIn company page into CSV/JSON. Buildable by DOM scraping LinkedIn's public pages. Demand spike from LinkedIn's increasingly restricted API makes client-side scraping tools valuable.
entries.pain: Sales reps manually screenshot or copy-paste employee data from LinkedIn profiles to build account org charts.

entries.title: Sitemap Health Monitor
entries.notes: Crawls a site's XML sitemap and checks every URL for HTTP errors, redirect chains, and missing meta tags. Simple to build with a background service worker crawler. Freemium for 100 pages monthly, paid for unlimited crawling and alerting.
entries.pain: SEO specialists lose ranking due to broken internal links, redirect chains, and missing canonical tags they only discover after drops.

entries.title: Cron Expression Visualizer
entries.notes: Highlights cron strings on any page and renders a human-readable timeline showing next 10 run times. Built with the popular cron-parser library and a custom timeline renderer. Small devtools utility with high utility density for anyone touching scheduled jobs.
entries.pain: Developers misread cron expressions and schedule jobs to run at wrong times, causing data inconsistencies or missed SLAs.

entries.title: Email Template Variable Extractor
entries.notes: Scrapes common email template providers (Gmail, HubSpot, Outreach) to auto-extract {{variable}} patterns and categorize them. Simple text parsing with provider-specific DOM workarounds. Low competition in extension form versus web apps.
entries.pain: Sales ops teams manually count and categorize merge fields across dozens of email templates to build variable inventories.

entries.title: Console Log Pattern Analyzer
entries.notes: Aggregates console output during development sessions and surfaces repeated patterns, grouping similar messages and surfacing anomalies. Buildable with console method interception and basic TF-IDF similarity scoring. Useful daily driver for any JS developer.
entries.pain: Frontend developers drowning in browser console noise struggle to find the 3 meaningful errors amid 200 routine logs.

entries.title: Lottie Animation Inspector
entries.notes: Detects Lottie JSON payloads on any page and renders a devtools panel showing layer structure, frame counts, and playback controls. Ships as a devtools extension leveraging Lottie's open-source player. Gap in browser devtools for this popular animation format.
entries.pain: Frontend developers debugging Lottie animations waste time manually inspecting JSON payload structure and frame data.

entries.title: Web Monetization Header Debugger
entries.notes: Inspects outgoing Web Monetization header presence and validates Coil/Wallet address formatting. Shows real-time payment stream status when visiting supported sites. Niche but growing with renewed interest in browser-based monetization alternatives.
entries.pain: Content creators using Web Monetization protocols have no easy way to verify their payment stream is actually working.

entries.title: API Response Time Benchmarker
entries.notes: Intercepts API calls and logs timing metrics, then displays a sortable table comparing endpoints across environments. Can tag requests with environment labels via URL patterns. Simple but fills a gap since most APM tools require server-side agents.
entries.pain: Backend developers comparing API latency across staging, QA, and production environments lack easy browser-side tooling.
